title: Khosla Ventures, first backer of OpenAI, is raising up to $5.5bn
excerpt: Vinod Khosla likes to be early. His firm was the first outside investor in OpenAI, back when that was a strange thing to be. Now Khosla Ventures is raising money to do it again, at a scale it has never tried. The firm is in talks to gather as much as $5.5bn across a new […]
content_html:
Vinod Khosla likes to be early. His firm was the first outside investor in OpenAI, back when that was a strange thing to be. Now Khosla Ventures is raising money to do it again, at a scale it has never tried.

The firm is in talks to gather as much as $5.5bn across a new set of funds, Bloomberg reported. People familiar with the private talks described the plans. That would be its largest raise in two decades. Its last set of funds, closed a year ago, came to around $4bn.

Most of the money would go where Khosla likes it: early. The plan floats about $1bn for seed-stage startups and roughly $2bn for slightly older ones. A further $2.5bn would form an “opportunity” fund for more mature companies.

Those are the ones now raising sums that once sounded absurd. A spokesperson declined to comment, and the figures could still move.

The 💜 of EU tech

The latest rumblings from the EU tech scene, a story from our wise ol' founder Boris, and some questionable AI art. It's free, every week, in your inbox. Sign up now!

The number is big, but the pattern is the story. Firms that got into OpenAI or Anthropic early are now raising the biggest funds of their lives. Paper gains have emboldened them, and they are racing to repeat the trick.

A stampede of record funds

The comparisons pile up. Last month Menlo Ventures closed a record $3bn, and it sits on a roughly $14bn stake in Anthropic. Peter Thiel’s Founders Fund recently raised $6bn, its largest ever. Earlier this year Sequoia gathered about $7bn. Each is a wager that the AI boom still has room to run, as Outlook Business noted.

Khosla’s own bets sketch the thesis. Beyond OpenAI, the firm seeded Cognition, an AI coding startup, and Sakana AI, which builds a language model for Japan. It also backed Physical Intelligence, which trains models for robots. The idea is that the next OpenAI is already somewhere in that list.

The man, not the football team

There was one other question on a recent fundraising call. Khosla had just led an investor group to buy the Seattle Seahawks for $9.6bn. That price would set a National Football League record.

On the call he assured investors he has no plans to run the team. He stays devoted to venture capital, Startup Fortune reported. That leaves the real question hanging over the raise. Can a firm catch the same lightning twice? Or is $5.5bn what the top of a market looks like?

Published July 23, 2026 - 1:34 pm UTC

Back to top

sentences.zh: 发布时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 1:34（世界标准时间）

content_html_zh:
维诺德·科斯拉 (Vinod Khosla) 喜欢早到。 他的公司是 OpenAI 的第一个外部投资者，当时这还是一件很奇怪的事情。 现在，Khosla Ventures 正在筹集资金再次开展这项业务，其规模是它从未尝试过的。

据彭博社报道，该公司正在洽谈通过一系列新基金募集高达 55 亿美元的资金。 熟悉私下会谈的人士描述了这些计划。 这将是二十年来最大的一次加薪。 其最后一批基金于一年前完成，规模约为 40 亿美元。

大部分资金都会流向科斯拉喜欢的地方：尽早。 该计划为种子期初创公司筹集了约 10 亿美元，为稍老的初创公司筹集了约 20 亿美元。 另外25亿美元将为更成熟的公司设立“机会”基金。

这些公司现在筹集的资金曾经听起来很荒谬。 发言人拒绝置评，而且这些数字仍可能发生变化。

欧盟科技的💜

来自欧盟科技界的最新传言、我们明智的创始人鲍里斯的故事，以及一些值得怀疑的人工智能艺术。 每周都会免费发送到您的收件箱中。 立即注册！

数字很​​大，但模式就是故事。 早期进入 OpenAI 或 Anthropic 的公司现在正在筹集一生中最大的资金。 账面收益让他们更加大胆，他们正竞相重蹈覆辙。

创纪录的资金蜂拥而至

比较堆积起来。 上个月，Menlo Ventures 的融资额达到创纪录的 30 亿美元，并持有 Anthropic 约 140 亿美元的股份。 Peter Thiel 的创始人基金最近筹集了 60 亿美元，这是有史以来规模最大的一次。 今年早些时候，红杉资本筹集了约 70 亿美元资金。 正如 Outlook Business 指出的那样，每一项都押注人工智能繁荣仍有发展空间。

科斯拉自己的赌注概述了这个论点。 除了 OpenAI 之外，该公司还投资了人工智能编码初创公司 Cognition 和为日本构建语言模型的 Sakana AI。 它还支持物理智能，为机器人训练模型。 我们的想法是，下一个 OpenAI 已经在该列表中的某个位置了。

是人，而不是足球队

最近的一次筹款电话会议还提出了另一个问题。 科斯拉刚刚带领一个投资集团以 96 亿美元收购了西雅图海鹰队。 这个价格将创下国家橄榄球联盟的纪录。

他在电话会议上向投资者保证，他没有计划管理该团队。 据 Startup Fortune 报道，他仍然致力于风险投资。 这使得真正的问题悬而未决。 一家公司能两次遭遇同一个闪电吗？ 或者说 55 亿美元就是市场顶部的样子？

发布时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日 - 下午 1:34（世界标准时间）

Back to top
